citations.description: test_build_fails_when_env_set_but_empty exists in test_build_provenance.py, not test_attestation_signing.py
Search for test_hook_fails_when_rebar_build_commit_empty and test_hook_falls_back_to_git_when_env_unset returned no matches across entire tests/ directory

findings.criterion: AC3: Both branches pinned by concrete tests in `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py` — (a) `test_hook_fails_when_rebar_build_commit_empty` runs `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT="" python -m build` in a temp copy of the tree and asserts non-zero exit; (b) `test_hook_falls_back_to_git_when_env_unset` builds with `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` unset in a git checkout and asserts the wheel carries the `git rev-parse --short HEAD` value.
findings.detail: Neither `test_hook_fails_when_rebar_build_commit_empty` nor `test_hook_falls_back_to_git_when_env_unset` exists anywhere in the codebase. (a) `test_attestation_signing.py` contains `test_resolve_build_commit_raises_on_set_but_empty_env` (lines 365–371) which tests the helper function directly — not a real `python -m build` invocation. The full-build fail-fast test (`test_build_fails_when_env_set_but_empty`) exists only in `test_build_provenance.py` under a different name. (b) No test exists anywhere that runs `python -m build` with `REBAR_BUILD_COMMIT` unset in a git checkout and verifies the wheel carries the `git rev-parse --short HEAD` short SHA — the AC's (b) branch is entirely absent. These tests were required to make the fail-fast/fallback contract codebase-verifiable in `test_attestation_signing.py`; both are missing.

citations.description: check (f): test_evidence_fragments_and_consolidation — does not include evidence-artifacts
check (i): test_i_build_once_bundle_with_sha256sums — asserts evidence-artifacts but is not check (f)

findings.criterion: AC4: `test_release_workflow_pins.py`'s check (f) (`test_evidence_fragments_and_consolidation`) is extended to assert that the `evidence-artifacts` fragment name + its wiring appear in `release.yml`.
findings.detail: The check (f) function `test_evidence_fragments_and_consolidation` (lines 128–133) iterates over `('evidence-shapin', 'evidence-build', 'evidence-mcp')` but does NOT include `evidence-artifacts`. The assertion for `evidence-artifacts` appears only in the separate check (i) function `test_i_build_once_bundle_with_sha256sums` (lines 184–194). The AC explicitly requires check (f) to be extended; this was not done — the assertion landed in a different function.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
